subject: RFI-187: Lobby east wall finish at Riverside Tower
body:
Hi,

Looking at the lobby east wall on A-201, the spec calls for honed limestone but the elevation shows a fluted pattern that isn't typical of honed stone. Can you confirm the intended finish, or should we go with custom fluted GFRC as an alternate? Owner had asked about budget options at the last meeting.

Need to lock this down so we can release the long-lead order. Trying to keep the curtainwall package on schedule.

Thanks,
Sara

Spec 095113 calls for high-NRC tile in Music 142 but the RCP shows the same grid pattern as the adjacent corridor. Acoustic engineer wants to clarify - is it a continuous grid with a tile change, or should the music room have its own framed soffit per the typical acoustic spaces?

Doesn't affect rough-in but we need to lock this before the ACT order goes in.

Please find the Phase 1 curtainwall shop drawings for Riverside Tower (CW-001 through CW-024) covering levels 2-8 of the north and east elevations. Submitted via SubmittalExchange this morning.

subject: Maplewood Senior Living - PCO #14 Unforeseen rock at south wing footings
body:
PM,

During excavation for the south wing footings F-12 through F-18, our crews encountered solid rock approximately 3' below the planned bearing elevation. This was not indicated on the geotech report, which we've re-pulled and shared with our PE for review.

We need a directive to proceed. Options:
1. Rock removal via mechanical means - 4 days, est. $48,500
2. Re-design footing to bear on rock with structural review - PE estimate pending

We are stopped at F-12 through F-18. Continuing other foundation work in parallel but this is on the critical path within 7 days.

Please advise.

Carlos Ramirez
Alliance Construction

subject: FYI - building dept inspector confirmed for Riverside Tower 5/19
body:
Just confirming - the building dept inspector (Reese) is coming for the L4 framing inspection on Tue 5/19 at 10 AM. Jamie is set up to receive. I'll be on site.

subject: RFI-119: Bay Street - rebar lap at column footings
body:
Quick one - S-202 shows #8 vertical bars at columns C-3, C-4, C-5 with a 36" lap to the footing dowels. Spec section 033000 says 'per ACI lap schedule' which would be 50" for #8 in compression. Which governs?

We're ready to tie tomorrow. Need to know to order any extra bar.

Tony

subject: RFI-241: Conflict at duct routing above Level 3 corridor
body:
Hi,

We've got a clash between the supply duct and the sprinkler main above the L3 corridor near Grid D/7. Both are showing in coordination but the sprinkler is fixed by sloped requirements. Can we drop the duct by 4" or do we need to re-route? Affects ceiling height in two rooms if we drop.

Drawing M-302 attached.

Needs an answer by Friday to keep MEP rough-in on schedule.

Thanks,
Devin

1. Safety moment

3. Open RFI status
4. Submittal log
5. Pending change orders
6. Owner items - lobby finish selection
7. Subcontractor coordination - curtainwall + glazing handoff

Brian

subject: INCIDENT REPORT - Bay Street - fall arrest anchor failure 5/14
body:
PM and team,

Reporting a Class B safety incident at Bay Street this morning at approximately 11:10 AM.

An ironworker (M. Castillo, ASW Steel) was working on roof joist installation at the south bay. His fall arrest lanyard anchor pulled free from a temporary clamp on a joist top chord. He fell approximately 4 feet to the joist below and was caught by his harness. No injuries.

Work stopped immediately. ASW Steel safety lead and our team conducted an on-the-spot investigation. The anchor was a temp clamp not rated for the load. Root cause: improper anchor selection. All fall arrest anchors site-wide are being re-inspected today.

Formal report will follow. OSHA has been notified per company policy.

Dave Hartmann
Safety Officer, Alliance Construction

body:
Crews: 16. Weather: cloudy, 65F.
- SOG Bay 6 prep continuing
- Joist installation south bay - paused after fall arrest incident (see Dave's report)

subject: Bay Street - PCO #08 - Unforeseen gas line at north property line
body:
PM,

During excavation for the north property line stormwater run, we encountered an active 4" gas service that is not shown on any drawing provided to us. Utility company confirmed the line is active and serves the adjacent property (was abandoned per our as-builts but is in fact live).

Work stopped at northing 1340. We are coordinating with the utility for safe relocation but I need a directive for the change order. Estimated impact:
- Cost: $34,000 (relocation + rework)
- Schedule: 6 working days delay to north stormwater install

Full PCO package follows tomorrow.

Marcus

subject: RFI-156: Highland Campus - moment frame connection at G/3
body:
PM,

The contractor is asking about the moment connection at column G/3 (drawing S-301). The detail shown is callout 4/S-501 which is a fully welded W14 connection. The W14 has been substituted with a W12 per our approved alternate of 4/22.

We need to either:
1. Re-issue 4/S-501 sized for W12
2. Field-verify the existing detail still meets demand at the reduced section

No schedule impact yet but they want this before tying rebar in two weeks.

James

The district superintendent's calendar shifted and the planned owner walkthrough on 5/22 needs to move. Can we look at 5/29 same time (10 AM) or alternatively 6/3 afternoon? She would like to see the music room ceiling and the gym floor in particular.

subject: Riverside Tower - Notice of impact - steel erection delay
body:
PM,

Writing to formally notify the project of an impact to Apex Steel's scope at Riverside Tower.

Per our subcontract, the slab at L3 was to be available for shake-out and crane positioning by 5/4/2026. As of today (5/15), L3 shoring is still in place and the slab has not been released for our use. This is 11 working days past the contracted handover date.

We are incurring standby costs for crew, crane rental, and shake-out coordination. Our preliminary impact estimate is $42,000 to date with additional costs accruing daily.

We request a meeting to discuss recovery and a formal change order to cover delays not caused by Apex. This letter serves as notice per Section 8.3 of our subcontract.

Please confirm receipt.

Mike Reyes
Project Manager, Apex Steel

Setting up the pull-planning session for the curtainwall + MEP + finishes interface on Riverside L2-L8.

body:
Hey, wanted to give you a heads up - one of our laborers slipped on the L5 deck during the wet morning yesterday. He's fine, walked off, complained about his shoulder a little but he was back on site this morning. Not a recordable, just a slip on wet rebar.

On another note we had to stop the L5 pour this morning - mat had standing water in three spots after that rain. We pumped it out and we'll pour Monday instead. No biggie.

Jamie

subject: RFI-242: Riverside Tower - fire alarm device count at L2 corridor
body:
Hi,

Device count on the L2 fire alarm shop drawings doesn't match the addressable loop count specified by Code Consultants. We show 22 devices on FA-A but the loop limit per their report is 20 (1 spare). Either we split the loop or remove 2 devices.

Proposing split with a new FACP module. Cost neutral if approved by Monday.

Let us know.

Aisha

subject: Lobby finish - going with the fluted GFRC
body:
Sara,

Thanks for walking me through the options yesterday. I think the fluted GFRC is the right call - the warm grey tone you showed in the second sample is what we want, with the satin sealer (not matte).

Please coordinate with the GC and curtainwall sub on the transition detail.

Also - I think it'd be fair to credit the limestone we're no longer using back to the project. Can you get the GC to issue a credit change order for the original limestone allowance and add this to the construction contract? We should probably do that this week so it doesn't slip.

Thanks,
David

subject: Bay Street - communication and coordination concerns
body:
PM,

I am writing to formally register concerns regarding communication and coordination on the Bay Street Industrial project.

Over the past two weeks, we have received notice of (1) an undocumented active gas line affecting the stormwater scope, (2) a near-miss fall incident, and (3) a missed underground utility coordination meeting. None of these were communicated to ownership in a timely manner. We learned about the gas line from our utility provider directly, not from the project team.

We expect:
1. Written notification of any work stoppages within 4 business hours
2. Owner attendance (or at minimum a written summary) for all utility coordination meetings
3. A communication plan documented and provided to us by 5/22

We value the relationship but the current pattern is not acceptable. Please confirm receipt and a path forward.

Janet Murray
Project Director, Bay Street Industrial

subject: Re: Highland Campus - Glass quote for Pavilion (Rev 2)
body:
Hi,

Quick follow-up. Our July production capacity is filling fast. If we don't have an intent letter by Tuesday 5/20 we'd have to push to August, which would put delivery into mid-October. I want to flag this so you can plan.

No new info on pricing, still good through 6/13.

Let me know.

Rachel

subject: Westfield - clarification on wood door specifications
body:
Hi,

We've been talking with the owner and they're concerned the wood doors as currently specified (HPDL face with hardwood edge banding) won't hold up to the wear they'll see in a K-12 environment. We want to clarify the spec to call for a solid hardwood face with edge trim - which we feel is more in line with the design intent. This is consistent with the language in 081416 paragraph 2.3.B which mentions 'durability appropriate for use.'

No drawing changes needed, just a spec interpretation. Can you have the GC adjust the submittal accordingly?

Lisa

subject: Maplewood Senior Living - PCO #15 Extended general conditions
body:
PM,

In light of the south wing rock issue (PCO #14) and other site conditions affecting the foundation schedule (delayed concrete deliveries 5/6-5/8), we are projecting a 14-working-day delay to foundation completion. This will extend the overall schedule by approximately the same.

We are submitting PCO #15 for extended general conditions:
- Office, trailer, dumpster, security: 14 days @ $1,800/day = $25,200
- Supervisor and field staff: $42,400
- Total: $67,600

Full backup attached. Request a directive within 5 business days to maintain crew commitment.

Carlos

subject: Westfield K-12 - quality concerns in Building A corridors
body:
PM,

At yesterday's site walk our facilities team noted several quality concerns in Building A:
- Visible drywall butt joints in three corridor segments (not flat at finished light)
- Door frame at room 112 is racked - measured 1/2" out of square
- Tile lippage in the staff restroom exceeds the spec tolerance in multiple locations

These were called out previously on the 4/30 walk but have not been corrected. The district is preparing to take partial occupancy of Building A in July and we cannot accept the work in its current state.

Please provide a written remediation plan with completion dates for each item by 5/22. Our facilities lead and I will inspect again 5/26.

Thank you,
Rob Tanaka
Westfield K-12 District

subject: For the record - call notes from this afternoon
body:
PM,

Following up on our call this afternoon, just so we have it written down. My notes:

- You acknowledged the slab handover is 11 days late
- You said you'd 'see what you could do' on the standby cost but couldn't commit

If I have anything wrong here please correct me. We're keeping the crew on site as requested.

Thanks,
Mike

subject: URGENT - Bay Street water main strike - need direction
body:
PM,

Not good news on a Saturday. Around 09:00 the operator on the south parking storm work hit an unmarked 6" water main. Water flow was active. Site flooded approximately 8,000 SF before utility could shut off.

Utility has shut off service. We have crews coming in tomorrow (Sunday) to begin pump out and assess. Damage to date includes:
- Site flooding (saturated subgrade)
- Wash-out of stormwater trench backfill
- Possible impact to recently installed underground electrical

We need:
1. Authorization for emergency response crews (committed pending your approval, est. $25K Sat+Sun)
2. PCO process to begin Monday
3. Owner notification - I left a voicemail with Janet's office

I tried reaching you by phone. Please respond ASAP.

Marcus

subject: SAFETY INCIDENT - Maplewood Senior Living - injury 5/16
body:
PM,

Reporting a Class A safety incident at Maplewood Senior Living, Saturday 5/16 at approximately 08:45 AM.

A framing carpenter (J. Olsen, Pacific Framing) was struck in the face by a 2x4 that kicked back from a circular saw. He suffered a laceration to the cheek and possible orbital fracture. He was transported by ambulance to St. Vincent's. As of this report he is being assessed.

Work stopped on the south wing. Photo evidence and witness statements being collected. OSHA notified per protocol. Full report by EOD Monday.

Dave Hartmann

subject: Quote follow-up - ML mechanical equipment
body:

Following up on the mechanical equipment quote for Maplewood Senior Living we sent two weeks ago. Quote is still valid through 5/30. Just looking for a yes/no on proceeding so we can either reserve production or release the slot.

subject: hey - water on the L2 slab again
body:
PM,

Morning. Quick one - we've got standing water on the L2 slab again, looks like 1/4" to 1/2" in patches over about 600 SF near the south curtainwall opening. Same area we mopped up Thursday. We covered the opening but rain must have come in over the weekend.

Let me know what you want me to do - we could just mop it again, but we're going to drywall this area Wed and three rounds of water in two weeks feels like we should be talking to someone (the architect? the curtainwall guy?). Don't want to be the one telling you in two months that the wall studs rusted.

Jamie

subject: Bay Street - Daily 5/18 + weekend update
body:
BS Daily and weekend update:

Weekend: emergency response to water main strike (Sat). Crews pumped and assessed Sun. Subgrade saturated as expected, drying with sun and pumps. Underground electrical conduit appears intact - to be verified Mon.

Mon plan:
- Continue pump out and assessment
- Megger test underground electrical
- Awaiting utility on repair schedule
- Bay 6 SOG pour pushed again - target Wed if subgrade dry

Marcus

body:
Hi,

The condenser pads on M-301 are shown clustered against the north wall of the building. Owner's facilities team flagged this conflicts with their planned forklift staging area shown on the OFCI site plan.

Proposing relocation to the NE yard - similar refrigerant lengths, no significant cost impact. Need architect/engineer signoff and owner buy-in.

Following the water main incident Saturday, submitting PCO #09 covering emergency response and schedule impact.

- Pump rental + standby Mon-Wed: $6,200
- Subgrade rework (estimated): $18,000

Schedule impact: 5-7 working day delay to southern site work; some critical-path implications for the building pad release.

Full backup follows. Need PM and owner sign-off on the emergency response cost ASAP and a directive on whether to expedite (additional ~$30K to recover schedule).

Marcus

subject: RFI-088: Maplewood Senior Living - bearing wall at corridor C-2
body:
PM,

GC asked about the bearing wall callout at corridor C-2 grid line 4. S-104 shows it as a load-bearing wall but the architectural plan A-105 has a 36" opening with a header that's not on our drawings. Need to add a header schedule or change the wall type.

Likely a coordination gap from the last set. We can issue a header detail quickly - just need direction. Nothing on the field schedule until after L1 walls in 2 weeks, so no rush.

James

subject: Highland Campus - Daily 5/18
body:
HC Daily 5/18:
- Crews: 24
- Paint finish coats Bldg B classrooms 201-208 complete
- Tile setters on L2 restrooms
- Millwork install L2 admin started
- Punch list Bldg A walked this AM, 84 items identified
- Landscape east berm 95%

No issues.

Megan

subject: Riverside Tower - introductory note
body:
Good morning,

My name is Andrea Sloane and I represent a contractor on the Riverside Tower project (Apex Steel). I'm writing to introduce myself and to make sure we have a contact at your firm in case any questions arise.

I also wanted to ask if you could share, when convenient, the project's notice provisions and the schedule of values for the steel package. Apex has provided their version but it would be helpful to compare with what your team has on file.

Nothing urgent. Look forward to working together.

Best regards,
Andrea Sloane
Kingsley Law LLP

subject: Quick question on the beam pour sequence
body:
Hey, quick one. The crew flagged that the rebar on the L4 transfer beams looks lighter than what we did at L3. Probably nothing but I wanted to ask before we pour Thursday morning. Drawings I have are S-204 rev 2, dated Feb 11. If there's a newer rev let me know.

Not urgent, just want to be sure.

Mike

subject: Riverside Tower - Apex Steel claim - follow-up
body:
PM,

Further to my notice of 5/15 regarding the slab handover delay at Riverside Tower, and our subsequent call, we have not received the formal response required under Section 8.4 of our subcontract.

We are placing the project on formal notice that we will be pursuing the full impact claim through our counsel beginning 5/22 unless we reach a written resolution. Our preliminary documented costs are now $51,400 with daily accrual.

We would prefer to resolve this directly. Please confirm a meeting time this week.

Mike Reyes
Apex Steel Project Manager

subject: [Highland Campus] Submittal status update
body:
Automated update - Highland Campus

3 submittals returned this week:
- 092900-002 Drywall - Approved
- 099000-003 Paint - Approved as Noted
- 230500-002 HVAC - Revise & Resubmit

1 new submittal uploaded:
- 095113-005 Acoustic ceiling tile (alternate)

Do not reply.

subject: ML - Updated foundation schedule + a quick note on south wing footings
body:
PM,

Quick update on the Maplewood foundation schedule. Attached is the revised 3-week look-ahead showing:
- North wing footings F-1 through F-11 complete
- East wing: target 5/29
- South wing: re-sequenced around the rock condition, target 6/12 (was 5/22)

For the south wing, we proceeded with mechanical rock removal at F-12 through F-18 as discussed. We also adjusted the bearing elevation at F-15 and F-16 by ~14" deeper based on field conditions - PE will issue a revised footing detail this week. Additional concrete approximately 26 CY across those two.

Let me know if you have questions.

Carlos

subject: RFI-244: Westfield - duct schedule discrepancy gymnasium
body:
Hi,

Duct schedule on M-302 shows 24x14 supply at the gym but the load calculation in 230593 supports 30x18. Either the load calc is wrong or the duct schedule is. Need engineer clarification before fab next week.

Devin

subject: RT Daily 5/18 PM
body:
Afternoon update:
- L5 deck pour completed 14:15, 760 CY, no rejected loads. Some heat at end of pour but within spec.
- MEP L3 walls 75%
- Curtainwall mockup teardown complete, permanent install starts Wed
- 1 first aid - laborer scraped hand on rebar, treated on site

Good day overall.

Jamie

subject: Re: ML Submittal 081100-001 Doors - returned with comments
body:
PM,

Checking in - haven't seen the resubmittal for the doors come through SubmittalExchange yet. The fire-rated mech room doors are on the critical path. We can't approve any of the package until those three doors are corrected. Need them back from the sub no later than 5/29.

Tom

subject: FYI - L4 rebar inspection passed (RT)
body:
Quick FYI - L4 deck rebar inspection at Riverside passed with no exceptions. Inspector Reese signed off, copy filed.

Anya

subject: NEAR-MISS - Highland Campus - falling debris on L3 catwalk
body:
PM,

Reporting a near-miss at Highland Campus 5/18 at approximately 15:20.

During removal of temporary lighting on the L3 catwalk, a 6' length of conduit fell from the catwalk to the floor below, landing approximately 4 feet from a tile setter. No injury. No damage.

Root cause: hardware not secured before disconnection. Stand-down conducted with all trades on L3. Tomorrow we'll re-train on overhead work permit procedures.

Not a recordable but documenting per company policy. Full report by EOW.

Dave

subject: Updated quote - Riverside Tower elevator maintenance contract
body:
PM,

Attached is our updated service quote for the post-handover elevator maintenance contract at Riverside Tower. Year 1 is covered under warranty - this quote is for Years 2 through 5.

Annual cost: $14,800 (3 cabs)
Includes monthly inspection, all PMs, 24/7 emergency response.

No action needed until closer to handover. Sending for files.

Gail / Vertic Elevator

subject: Weekly internal PM call - tomorrow Tue 5/19 9 AM
body:
Reminder - weekly internal PM call tomorrow Tue 5/19 at 9 AM. Standard agenda: project-by-project status, blockers, financial check-in. If you have items for the agenda send by 8 AM.

Diane

subject: Riverside Tower - Pay App #14 rejected
body:
PM,

We are rejecting Pay Application #14 as submitted for the following reasons:

1. Stored material claim of $84,000 for curtainwall components - we have no evidence these materials are on a bonded site.
2. Front-loaded labor on Division 03 (concrete) - the schedule of values appears to overstate completion versus what we observed on the site walk 5/8.
3. PCO line items included that have not been approved by ownership (PCO-009, PCO-011).

We will withhold $187,000 from the application pending resolution. We would like to schedule a meeting this week with the project executive to discuss.

David

subject: FYI - ML door schedule rev 4 issued
body:
Heads up - door schedule revision 4 issued today. Captures the fire-rated mech room changes and a few minor frame corrections. Coordinated with the hardware spec. Posted to SharePoint.

No action required unless you spot something off.

Tom

subject: Bay Street - Daily 5/19 (AM)
body:
Quick Tuesday start. Subgrade dry-out continuing. Megger test on underground electrical scheduled this morning. Utility crew on site by 10 for water main repair. Bay 6 SOG pour now target Thursday.

Full daily later.

Marcus
